LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC, U.S. BANK N.A.'S MOTION TO VACATE THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT TAKEN AGAINST IT ON AUGUST 23, 2019 IS GRANTED, AND THE HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT SET FOR DECEMBER 10, 2019 IN DEPT. 514 IS VACATED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) DEFENDANT SHALL FILE ITS ANSWER OR OTHER RESPONSIVE PLEADING WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance via CourtCall:
C. Todd Norris, Esq., (415)957-3307, appearing for Specialized, Defendant.

During the course of the ex parte calendar, Mr. Norris explained his filings to staff attorney Ms. Newman. Furthermore, Mr. Norris stated that he had an application for pro hac vice that was calendar for today, Thursday, September 24, 2020 and that it had not been entered on the register of actions yet. Ms. Newman requested that Mr. Norris follow the filing guidelines for San Francisco county and explained that Alameda county has their own system.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE is granted. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-25-2019 Continued To Feb-05-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Off Calendar

Defendant Specialized Loan Servicing Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default; Exhibit A
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Off Calendar

Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default; Exhibit A
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC, U.S. BANK N.A.'S Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default.

Defendant Specialized Loan Servicing LLC's motion to vacate the entry of default taken against it on August 23, 2019 is granted, and the hearing on Plaintiff's motion for entry of default judgment set for December 10, 2019 in Dept. 514 is vacated. Defendant has shown that default was entered due to its mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect due to its failure to promptly assign the case to outside counsel as a result of short staffing and miscommunications in its legal department. (Sullivan Decl. 3-5, 7.) Further, the Court finds that Plaintiff's counsel acted unprofessionally by taking Defendant's default only one day after the deadline for it to file a responsive pleading, without providing any advance notice to Defendant. (See Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 135.) Finally, Plaintiff has not shown that it will suffer any prejudice if the motion is granted. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233 [where the party in default moves promptly to seek relief, and no prejudice to the opposing party will result from setting aside the default and letting the case go to trial on the merits, "very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside the default."].) Accordingly, the motion is granted. Defendant shall file its answer or other responsive pleading within 10 days of this order.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
